tax_note: Federal + Provincial tax; GST/HST registration required above $30K revenue

vat_gst.name: GST/HST
vat_gst.rate_percent: 5% GST federal; 13-15% HST in harmonized provinces (ON, NB, NL, NS, PE)
vat_gst.registration_threshold: $30,000 in revenue over four consecutive calendar quarters
vat_gst.registration_threshold_note: Below $30K, GST/HST registration is optional. Voluntary registration lets you claim input tax credits on business purchases.
vat_gst.digital_products: Digital products sold to Canadian consumers are subject to GST/HST. Non-resident digital businesses must register if Canadian sales exceed $30K. Quebec has its own QST registration requirement.

digital_product_tax: Digital products are subject to GST/HST. If you sell to customers in Quebec, separate QST (9.975%) registration is also required above $30K in Quebec sales. Saskatchewan charges PST on digital products. Use a tax automation service if selling across provinces.

entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Business income added to personal return. Federal + provincial rates combined (20-53%). CPP contributions on self-employment income.
entity_comparison.best_for: Starting out; testing a business idea; revenue under $40K

entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Small business deduction: 9% federal on first $500K of active business income. Combined federal+provincial rate ~12-15% for small businesses. Salary or dividends for personal income extraction.
entity_comparison.best_for: Revenue above $50K/year; the 9% small business rate makes incorporation very attractive in Canada

isp_note: Rogers/Bell fibre in urban areas; limited rural options
disclaimer: This is general information only and does not constitute tax, legal, or financial advice. Tax law varies by province. Consult a Canadian CPA for advice specific to your situation.
